docs/helix/02-design/workers-sessions-operator-surface.md
docs/helix/02-design/plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture.md

80	- **Execution evidence required**: The bead must carry one of:
81	  - A `closing_commit_sha` (exact SHA of the commit that closed the work)
82	  - A `session_id` (agent session that ran the work)
83	  - An execute-bead success event in the events history
84	  - AND a terminal verdict event (review APPROVE with non-empty rationale, explicit review-skipped marker, or manual-close marker)
85	- **ClosureGate enforcement**: `CloseWithEvidence` enforces `ClosureGate` (per `ddx-e30e60a9`) to reject closes without sufficient evidence, preventing silent false-closures. `Store.Close` bypasses the gate by design as a manual-administration escape hatch.
86	- **Code locations**: `store.go:1568-1610` (`CloseWithEvidence`), `store.go:1428-1560` (`Store.Close`), `store.go:1502-1558` (`ClosureGate` definition and documentation).
87	
88	Both paths append an event (`lifecycle_reconciled` for reconcile-close, implicit in the session evidence for evidence-close) and trigger event externalization to sidecars when needed.
89	
90	### 3.1.1 Lifecycle Reliability Invariants
91	
92	- Raw `no_changes` is attempt evidence, not a durable bead queue state. The drain loop MUST translate it into one of the rows below before mutating the bead.
93	- `work-retry-after` may be set only when retrying the same bead after time passes could plausibly succeed without human/spec/dependency changes.
94	  - **MUST NOT set retry-after** (cause is not time-resolvable by waiting): `push_failed`, `declined_needs_decomposition`, `review_spec_gap`, `review_missing_acceptance`, `review_too_large` at decomposition depth cap.
95	  - **MAY set retry-after** (recheckable by waiting): `push_conflict` (15 min — remote advanced), `land_conflict` (15 min), transient quota/transport (15 min). `no_viable_provider` MUST NOT set per-bead retry-after when alternate routing paths exist or when the worker can transition to `paused-infra`; a per-bead cooldown is only appropriate when no alternate route exists AND the condition is purely time-dependent with no other worker that could claim the bead.
96	  - Cooldown lifetime SHOULD match the recheckable window; 15 min is the ceiling for every current recheckable outcome. A 24 h cooldown is never appropriate.
97	- Continuous forward progress is the default. Before a bead can move to
98	  `status=proposed`, the drain loop MUST exhaust every applicable automatic
99	  path in priority order: same-result review retry, same-bead implementation
100	  retry, reframe, decomposition, sibling/replacement split, or already-satisfied
101	  verification. `status=proposed` is only valid when those paths failed, would
102	  be lossy, or require a product/spec choice an agent cannot infer.
103	- `status=blocked` is reserved for hard external recheckable blockers. Too-large
104	  work, decomposition depth overflow, exhausted implementation retries,
105	  exhausted reviewer retries, and no-changes uncertainty MUST NOT map to
106	  `blocked` unless the evidence names an external blocker whose later resolution
107	  can be rechecked mechanically.
108	- `parent` and `deps` are disjoint structural relationships. A bead's `deps`
109	  MUST NOT include its parent or any bead on its parent chain. Taken together
110	  with the `parent` links, dependency edges MUST remain acyclic with respect to
111	  the parent tree so the structural graph cannot fold back on itself.
112	- Decomposition depth overflow is not, by itself, an operator-required
113	  condition. At child-depth cap, the decomposer MUST try a sibling or replacement
114	  split under the nearest safe parent/root. Only a failed or lossy
115	  sibling/replacement split may map to `status=proposed`.
116	- Every bead excluded from ordinary `ddx work` execution MUST have an explainable durable reason using existing mechanisms: dependency edge, `proposed` status, external `blocked` status, `execution-eligible=false`, `superseded-by`, epic/parent queue mode, or an active retry cooldown. Labels, events, and `extra` fields may explain that reason but do not control lifecycle.
117	- `extra` is not a general rule namespace. Only fields explicitly specified by
118	  TD-027 or this TD may affect queue eligibility, retry/recovery thresholds, or
119	  worker selection. Optional telemetry in `extra` may improve auditability, but
120	  missing optional telemetry MUST NOT by itself block, propose, cooldown, or
121	  skip otherwise executable work.
122	- `closed` means implementation, verification, and the default adversarial pre-close review gate have all passed, or the work was verified as already satisfied. Review failure never reopens a closed bead; it prevents close.
123	- Automatic implementation retry is bounded and applies only to classifications where the implementer had valid task context and further automated work can plausibly resolve the finding. Spec gaps, missing acceptance criteria, decomposition overflow, and exhausted reviewer failures require operator resolution only after the automatic reframe/decompose/replacement sequence cannot produce executable work.
124	- Latest terminal events and close evidence beat stale `work-*` `extra` metadata. Reconciliation may clear stale management fields, but MUST preserve append-only events and evidence.
125	- Closure paths are covered in detail in §3.1: reconcile-close (automatic, dependency-driven) vs evidence-close (manual or execution-driven). See §3.1 for the safety and evidence requirements of each path.
126	
127	### 3.1.2 Try-Loop Attempt State Machine
128	
129	`ddx try` and the per-bead attempt inside `ddx work` use a small in-memory
130	state machine before any durable bead lifecycle mutation. The implementation
131	entrypoint is `cli/internal/agent/executeloop.DecideAttemptTransition`; CLI and
132	server workers MUST call the same transition function so retry behavior does
133	not drift between execution modes.
134	
135	The try-loop states are control-flow states only. They do not add persisted bead
136	statuses.
137	
138	| State | Input | Transition | Action |
139	|---|---|---|---|
140	| `classify_result` | `success`, `already_satisfied`, `no_changes`, structural validation, or any non-escalatable status | `stop_attempt` | Return the report to the lifecycle mapper in §3.2. |
141	| `classify_result` | disrupted report, budget exhaustion, or a hard passthrough/operator constraint | `stop_attempt` | Return the report; do not raise `MinPower`. |
142	| `classify_result` | capability-sensitive failure such as implementation failure, post-run check failure, or review block | `retry_power` when the power ladder has a higher viable floor; otherwise `stop_attempt` | Retry the same bead with only `MinPower` raised to the next viable catalog power. Preserve policy intent and all operator pins. |
143	| `classify_result` | provider-connectivity infrastructure failure with concrete route evidence (`actual_power > 0`) and no operator harness/provider/model pin | `retry_power` | Retry immediately with `MinPower = actual_power + 1`, preserving policy intent. This asks Fizeau to route above the failed low-power route without DDx naming a tier, provider, or model. |
144	| `classify_result` | infrastructure failure without concrete route evidence, or with an operator harness/provider/model pin | `stop_attempt` | Record/release according to §3.2. A smarter model cannot fix an absent service when DDx has no alternate power signal, and pins must not be widened silently. |
145	| `retry_power` | retry finishes | `classify_result` | Reclassify the new report through the same table. |
146	| `stop_attempt` | report returned to worker | §3.2 outcome mapping | Apply the durable bead lifecycle mutation, event, and recovery policy. |
147	
148	`policy` remains routing intent. `MinPower` is only a retry/fallback bound after
149	DDx has attempt evidence. Initial zero-config dispatch MUST NOT duplicate the
150	selected policy's lower bound as `MinPower`; Fizeau owns the concrete route
151	within the selected policy and any numeric power bounds DDx sends.
152	
153	### 3.2 Outcome → Label / Event / Extra Mapping
154	
155	| Outcome or lifecycle action | Status transition | Label changes | Events appended | Extra updates |
156	|---|---|---|---|---|
157	| `review_pass` after merged candidate | `in_progress → closed` | remove `claimed`, add `last-merged-rev:<sha>` (optional) | `review-pass` + `closed-merged` | `extra.last-run`, `extra.last-review`, `extra.closing_commit_sha` updated; clear stale `work-*` |
158	| `already_satisfied` | `in_progress → closed` | (none) | `closed-already-satisfied` | `extra.last-run`; clear stale `work-*` |
159	| `review_fixable_gap` with retry budget remaining | no durable change if continuing; otherwise `in_progress → open` | no lifecycle label required | `review-fixable-gap` + retry decision | `extra.last-review` carries findings ref |
160	| `review_fixable_gap` with retry budget exhausted | `in_progress → open` to enter auto-recovery; `in_progress → proposed` only after `auto_recovery_failed` | optional explanatory review label only | `review-fixable-gap` + ladder-exhausted/recovery decision | `extra.last-review` carries findings ref and exhausted budget; may increment `extra.consecutive_ladder_exhaustions` |
161	| `review_spec_gap` / `review_missing_acceptance` | `in_progress → open` when a safe reframe can make the bead executable; `in_progress → proposed` only when the missing spec/AC requires operator judgment | add `triage:spec-gap` or `triage:missing-acceptance` | `review-block` + optional `reframe-applied` | `extra.last-review` carries findings ref; `extra.last-recovery` records any safe rewrite attempt |
162	| `review_too_large` | `in_progress → open` with child dep edges, or sibling/replacement dep edges at child-depth cap; `in_progress → proposed` only if decomposition is lossy or no executable split can be generated | add `decomposed` when children/replacements exist | `review-too-large` + optionally `triage-decomposed` / `triage-overflow` | `extra.children` or `extra.superseded-by` + AC mapping when decomposed |
163	| `review_error` below retry cap | no durable change; retry reviewer for same `result_rev` | (none) | `review-error` | `extra.last-review-error` carries class, slot, attempt count |
164	| `review_error` exhausted | `in_progress → open` unless the error proves operator action is required; `in_progress → proposed` only after the automatic review/recovery path is exhausted | optional explanatory review label only | `review-manual-required` only for operator-required classes; otherwise `review-error` + recovery decision | `extra.last-review-error` carries class, slot, attempt count |
165	| `execution_failed` | `in_progress → open` | (none) | `unclaimed` + structured failure event | `extra.last-run` |
166	| verified no_changes already satisfied | `in_progress → closed` | remove no_changes triage labels | `no_changes_verified` + `closed-already-satisfied` | `extra.last-run`; clear `work-*` |
167	| unverified no_changes | `in_progress → open` | add `triage:no-changes-unverified` | `no_changes_unverified` | record verification command/result; do not set retry cooldown by default |
168	| unjustified no_changes | `in_progress → open` | add `triage:no-changes-unjustified` | `no_changes_unjustified` | record rationale absence/detail |
169	| legacy no_changes investigation (work too large) | `in_progress → open` with child dep edges, or sibling/replacement dep edges at child-depth cap; `in_progress → proposed` only if decomposition is lossy or no executable split can be generated | add `decomposed` when children/replacements exist | legacy `no_changes_needs_investigation` + `triage-decomposed`/`triage-overflow` | `extra.last-rationale`, `extra.children` or `extra.superseded-by`, AC mapping |
170	| legacy no_changes investigation (non-decomposition reason) | `in_progress → open` for retriable, verifiable, or recoverable uncertainty; `in_progress → proposed` only when evidence proves operator judgment is required | optional explanatory triage labels only | legacy `no_changes_needs_investigation` + recovery decision | `extra.last-rationale`; no retry cooldown |
171	| parent/epic/decomposed container | `in_progress → open` with dep edges to children, or `in_progress → open` with `execution-eligible=false` | add `decomposed` when children exist | `no_changes_decomposed` or `triage-decomposed` | `extra.children` lists child IDs + AC mapping, or `extra.execution-eligible=false` |
172	| external blocker | `in_progress → blocked` (hard) or `in_progress → open` (soft) | add `blocked-on-upstream:<id>` as explanatory label when useful | `no_changes_blocked` | `extra.last-rationale` names the external blocker |
173	| superseded work | no terminal success; leave open if visible history needed | (none) | structured superseded event if appended | `extra.superseded-by` names the replacement |
174	| transient infra/quota/transport | `in_progress → open` | (none) | `no_changes_recoverable`, `drain-paused-quota`, `rate-limit-retry`, or structured transport event | may set `work-retry-after` for retryable time-based condition only |
175	| `push_failed` (branch protection, auth-token, pre-push test, executor race) | `in_progress → open` | optional explanatory label (e.g. `blocked-on-branch-protection`) | `push-failed` with stderr detail and base-rev | `extra.last-run` only; **DO NOT** set `work-retry-after` |
176	| `push_conflict` (remote advanced; FF rejected) | `in_progress → open` | (none) | `push-conflict` with current remote head | may set `work-retry-after` at 15 min |
177	| stale no_changes tracker metadata | no status change unless latest terminal evidence closes the bead | remove stale no_changes triage labels only when contradicted by terminal evidence | preserve historical events; append reconciliation event if performed | clear stale `work-*` when latest terminal event proves them obsolete |
178	| dependency-satisfied reconcile close (`ddx bead reconcile`, `CloseSatisfied=true`) | `open → closed` via `UpdateWithLifecycleStatus`, `ManualClose=true`; `ClosureGate` **not** invoked | remove stale `work-*` labels; add `reconciled-nochanges-state` label | `lifecycle_reconciled` | `extra.work-*` cleared per `ReconcilePlan.ClearFields`; `externalizeEvents` called after close |
179	| `reframe_applied` — reframer rewrote description/AC; bead re-enters execution-ready lane | `in_progress → open` | add `reframed` label | `reframe-applied` with `from_rev`, `to_rev`, `reframer_cost` | `extra.consecutive_ladder_exhaustions` reset to 0; `extra.last-recovery` updated |
180	| `decompose_applied` — decomposer filed 2-5 executable child, sibling, or replacement beads; oversized bead remains open but not directly executable | `in_progress → open` with child dep edges, sibling/replacement dep edges, or supersession metadata; oversized bead `execution-eligible=false` when it should not be claimed directly | add `decomposed` label | `decompose-applied` with generated bead IDs + `reframer_cost` | `extra.children` or `extra.superseded-by` + AC mapping; `extra.execution-eligible=false`; `extra.last-recovery` updated |
181	| `auto_recovery_failed` — reframe, child decomposition, and sibling/replacement decomposition failed, or a valid operator-authored `recovery:manual` label set | `in_progress → proposed` | add `triage:auto-recovery-failed` label | `auto-recovery-failed` with `reframe_attempt_cost` + `decompose_attempt_cost` | `extra.last-recovery` records costs and failure reasons; **DO NOT** set `work-retry-after` |
182	| `per_bead_budget_exhausted` — cumulative cost exceeded `escalation.per_bead_budget_usd` | `in_progress → open` (re-claimable — budget exhaustion is recheckable) | (none) | `per-bead-budget-exhausted` with `total_cost` | `extra.last-run` only; **DO NOT** set `work-retry-after` — requires operator action or config change |
183	
184	Legacy/backcompat `needs_human` and `triage:needs-investigation` labels are not lifecycle controls. New routing uses `status=proposed` for operator decisions; those labels may remain only as migration metadata until cleanup removes them.
185	
186	### 3.3 Decomposition-First Transition Sequence
187	
188	When any pre-claim readiness result, post-attempt triage result, review result,
189	or no-changes rationale classifies the bead as too large, needing breakdown, or
190	structurally non-executable, DDx applies this sequence before any
191	operator-attention transition:
192	
193	1. **Safe in-place reframe**: if the bead can be made executable by preserving
194	   commitments while tightening description or acceptance criteria, apply
195	   `reframe_applied`; transition `in_progress → open` or `open → open`; reset
196	   `extra.consecutive_ladder_exhaustions`.
197	2. **Child decomposition**: if the bead can be split under itself, create 2-5
198	   executable child beads, map every parent AC to child ACs or explicit
199	   `non_scope`, add dependency edges, set the parent
200	   `extra.execution-eligible=false`, add `decomposed`, append
201	   `decompose-applied`, and leave the parent `status=open`.
202	3. **Sibling/replacement decomposition**: if child depth is exhausted but the
203	   work is still decomposable, create executable sibling or replacement beads
204	   under the nearest safe parent/root, record `extra.superseded-by` on the
205	   oversized bead when a replacement owns the remaining work, add dependency
206	   edges so the queue advances through the replacement work, and leave the
207	   oversized bead `status=open` but not execution-eligible.
208	4. **Final escape**: move to `status=proposed` only when reframe,
209	   child decomposition, and sibling/replacement decomposition all fail, would
210	   drop explicit scope, or require operator judgment. The event body MUST record
211	   which automatic actions were attempted and why each could not safely move
212	   work forward.
213	
214	This sequence is synchronous from the state-machine perspective: there is no
215	durable "needs decomposition" status. If implementation needs to release a claim
216	between steps, it releases to `status=open` with evidence naming the pending
217	automatic recovery action, not to `blocked`, cooldown, or `proposed`.
218	
219	## 4. Worker-State Enumeration

251:- **Threshold**: when `consecutive_ladder_exhaustions >= 2` (default; configurable in `ddxroot.Path()/config.yaml` as `escalation.auto_recovery_threshold`), the drain loop triggers the auto-recovery sequence described in ADR-024 Escalation Sequencing and SD-025 Layer 3.5.
285:For successful replacement rewrites, the bead body may be materially shorter or longer than the original when prompt fitness requires it. Preservation is proven by the `triage-rewritten` / `intake-rewritten` evidence record and durable anchors, not by keeping old text inside the prompt body. For rejected rewrites, readiness enters the §3.3 decomposition-first sequence when structural follow-up work can preserve explicit scope; it moves the bead to `status=proposed` only when safe rewrite/decomposition/replacement cannot resolve the ambiguity without operator judgment.
333:**Filesystem-shape contract**: per `ddx-d30bc1a0`, the main-git/tracker lock path is `ddxroot.Path()/.git-tracker.lock`, where `ddxroot.Path()` is the resolved per-project DDx root. It is a process-shared lock **directory**, not a regular lockfile. Lock acquisition MUST classify the existing path immediately after `mkdir` reports that it already exists and MUST NOT sleep/back off until the path is confirmed to be a real lock directory.
384:State paths in this document use `ddxroot.Path()` (shorthand for `ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)`) per `ddx-06cbaa90` (presence-based DDx root resolution and per-project worktree registry).

55	### Storage Location
56	
57	Runtime state is host+user scoped:
58	
59	```
60	~/.local/share/ddx/            (XDG_DATA_HOME/ddx if set)
61	  server-state.json            node identity + project registry
62	  server.addr                  last-known server URL + PID
63	```
64	
65	State that describes the server instance never lives inside one project's
66	`ddxroot.Path()` directory. The addr file and state file both belong to the operator
67	(the user running the server), not to any project. One server per machine:
68	the addr file is a single-entry file; a new server start overwrites it.
69	
70	### Project Identity (ddx-d30bc1a0)
71	
72	Per `ddx-d30bc1a0`, every project-scoped DDx path in this topology is rooted at
73	`ddxroot.Path()`, shorthand here for `ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)`. In-tree
74	projects still resolve to `<projectRoot>/.ddx`; convention-mode projects
75	resolve to the per-project XDG directory derived from git remote identity (or
76	the documented local fallback). The host+user registry stores checkout paths and
77	resolved project IDs, but beads, workers, execution bundles, attachments, and
78	other project state remain under that project's own `ddxroot.Path()`.
79	
80	### Registry Population
81	
82	Three paths contribute entries:
83	
84	1. **Server startup** registers the project at the server's own working
85	   directory.
86	2. **CLI auto-registration** — `ddx run`, `ddx try`, `ddx work`, `ddx bead`,
87	   and `ddx doc` subcommand groups fire a fire-and-forget
88	   `POST /api/projects/register` in
89	   `PersistentPreRunE` so any project the user touches on the machine shows
90	   up in the host+user registry without manual configuration (FEAT-020).
91	3. **Optional config seed** — a `server.projects` block in config may list
92	   known roots so a fresh install can boot with a non-empty registry before
93	   any CLI command runs. Seed entries are merged into the persisted state,
94	   not kept as a separate source of truth.

input.command: cd cli 2>/dev/null; ddx bead show ddx-06cbaa90 2>&1 | head -50; echo "---"; ddx bead show ddx-d30bc1a0 2>&1 | head -50

data.output:
ID:       ddx-06cbaa90
Title:    ddx home: presence-based root resolution + per-project worktree registry
Type:     task
Status:   closed
Priority: 1
Labels:   area:config, area:refactor, initiative:zero-footprint, kind:refactor, phase:3, principle:ddx-home
Parent:   ddx-602ae31a
Deps:     ddx-77eb084e, ddx-5cc92777
Desc:     PROBLEM (Principle 1 of epic ddx-602ae31a)

Eliminate hardcoded .ddx/ paths so that DDx state anchors correctly regardless of whether the project has a .ddx/ folder. Today every code path joins filepath.Join(projectRoot, ".ddx", ...) directly, so introducing an XDG fallback or any other root strategy means a many-callsite rewrite.

DESIGN

ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot) is the single accessor. It returns:
- <projectRoot>/.ddx — when that directory exists (in-tree mode)
- ${XDG_DATA_HOME}/ddx/projects/<host>/<owner>/<repo>/ — otherwise (convention mode)

Project identity in convention mode is parsed from `git remote get-url origin`:
- https://github.com/easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- git@github.com:easel/ddx.git → github.com/easel/ddx
- https://gitlab.example.com/team/repo → gitlab.example.com/team/repo
- Local (no remote) → local/<basename>-<short-hash-of-abs-path>

The XDG project directory IS a git repository — `git init`ed on first bootstrap. All DDx state for that project (beads.jsonl, attachments, archive, plugins, etc.) lives there and is versioned. Operators can `git remote add` it to a server to share state with teammates.

WORKTREE REGISTRY (required, not speculative)

Each XDG project root maintains <xdg>/worktrees.json:

  {"paths": [{"path": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx", "first_seen_at": "...", "last_seen_at": "...", "hostname": "..."}], "master": "/Users/erik/Projects/ddx"}

- The first worktree to bootstrap an XDG project is auto-marked master.
- Subsequent worktrees register as non-master.
- `ddx project worktree list` shows the registry.
- `ddx project worktree master <path>` re-pins.
- Audit log entries (bead events with result_rev/base_rev) include the master path so downstream tools resolve commit SHAs to a known worktree.

DDX_ROOT NEVER PUNNED — bootstrap is atomic, idempotent, and a no-op when the directory already exists. Two concurrent ddx invocations on the same project must not race-create or race-write worktrees.json. Use file-locking (existing gitlock pattern from ddx-6a518fd9) where needed.

AUDIT — every Go file in cli/ that contains the literal ".ddx" as a path segment must route through ddxroot.Path. Touched packages at minimum:
- cli/cmd/ (every command that reads/writes state)
- cli/internal/agent/ (execute_bead*, work/, escalation/)
- cli/internal/bead/ (store, migrate, lifecycle)
- cli/internal/server/ (workers, federation, GraphQL adapters)
- cli/internal/config/ (resolved, types)
- cli/internal/triage/, cli/internal/metaprompt/, cli/internal/registry/
- library/checks/ (where ddx-side paths exist)

A regression linter (lefthook hook or go vet analyzer) blocks any PR that introduces a new filepath.Join(..., ".ddx", ...) literal outside cli/internal/ddxroot/.
---

Flags:

      --json   Output as JSON

Global Flags:
      --config string              config file (default is $HOME/.ddx.yml)
      --library-base-path string   override path for DDx library location
      --no-update-check            Disable the automatic update check (network call) on each command
  -v, --verbose                    verbose output

80	3. The machine's `ddx-server` registered with the project root so the land
81	   coordinator starts.
82	
83	No additional configuration is required. Machines do not need to reach each
84	other; they only need write access to the shared `origin`.
85	
86	## Project Identity (ddx-d30bc1a0)
87	
88	Per `ddx-d30bc1a0`, each machine resolves project-scoped DDx state through
89	`ddxroot.Path()`, shorthand here for `ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)`. For
90	multi-machine coordination, participating clones SHOULD share the same remote
91	identity so convention-mode resolution converges on the same
92	`<host>/<owner>/<repo>` project key. The no-remote local fallback is
93	intentionally machine-local and is not a cross-machine identity contract.
94	
95	That means each machine's coordinator reads and writes its own local
96	`ddxroot.Path()/beads.jsonl`, `ddxroot.Path()/executions/<attempt-id>/`, and
97	`ddxroot.Path()/worktrees.json`; the shared git remote coordinates landing, not
98	the project-state directory itself.
99	
100	## Happy Path
101	
102	1. Worker on machine A claims a bead (bead is marked claimed in local
103	   `ddxroot.Path()/beads.jsonl`; not yet visible on machine B).
104	2. Worker executes the bead in an isolated worktree.

135	- **Default power policy** is the project's configured policy.
136	- **Acceptance section** is auto-generated from a template (e.g.
137	  "agent must produce a diff or evidence rationale; no AC = AC is the
138	  prompt verbatim"). The structural AC check is skipped for this
139	  `issueType` — the structural check assumes pre-authored AC.
140	- **First event** records the identity-bound audit fields enumerated above.
141	- **Backlinks.** Every bead created/changed and every artifact mutated by
142	  an operator-prompt execution gets an `origin_operator_prompt_id` event
143	  so provenance survives later edits, rebases, and history rewrites.
144	
145	### Project Identity (ddx-d30bc1a0)
146	
147	Per `ddx-d30bc1a0`, the web write path is project-scoped before it is
148	prompt-scoped. The receiving node resolves the selected project's DDx root via
149	`ddxroot.Path()`, shorthand here for `ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)`, then
150	persists the operator-prompt bead under `ddxroot.Path()/beads.jsonl`,
151	attachments under `ddxroot.Path()/attachments/<bead-id>/`, and execution
152	evidence under `ddxroot.Path()/executions/<attempt-id>/`. This keeps
153	operator-prompt storage aligned across in-tree and convention-mode projects.
154	
155	## Multi-node delegation policy
156	
157	Per ADR-007 (federation topology), prompts run on the client node that owns
158	the project's queue, not on the coordinator. The submission flow:
159	
160	1. The operator's browser submits to whichever node it is connected to.
161	2. If that node owns the project's queue, it persists the bead locally and
162	   wakes the local work.
163	3. If the receiving node is a hub that does not own the queue, it forwards the
164	   submission to the owning spoke or managed node, **carrying the originating

data.output:
30	- Stop: visible only for workers in `running` state. The mutation calls the
31	  same `WorkerManager.Stop` path reached by `legacy agent workers stop`, so claim
32	  release, graceful cancellation, process-group termination, and stopped-state
33	  persistence stay centralized.
34	
35	The proposed "cancel current bead without killing the loop" control is not
36	implemented in this pass because there is no separate CLI/runtime primitive for
37	abandoning only the in-flight bead while preserving the loop process. Adding it
38	requires an work semantic change and remains out of scope for this
39	operator-surface pass.
40	
41	## Project Identity (ddx-d30bc1a0)
42	
43	Every worker and session view is project-scoped. Per `ddx-d30bc1a0`, worker
44	liveness sidecars live under `ddxroot.Path()/workers/<worker-id>/status.json`
45	and immutable run evidence lives under
46	`ddxroot.Path()/executions/<attempt-id>/`, where `ddxroot.Path()` is shorthand
47	for `ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)`. The server and UI must resolve project
48	context first, then read these paths, so the operator surface behaves the same
49	for in-tree and convention-mode projects.
50	
51	## Navigation and Audit
52	
53	Workers and Sessions link to each other:
54	
55	- Workers page header links to Recent sessions.
56	- Sessions page header links back to Workers.
57	- Worker detail lists sessions whose `workerId` matches the worker.
58	- Expanded session rows link back to the producing worker when `workerId` is
59	  present.
60	
61	Every lifecycle action performed through the server records a timestamped
62	worker lifecycle event with the server's single-operator actor identifier.
63	Those events are surfaced on the worker detail page beside the live response
64	and log tail, so an operator can see who started or stopped the process and
65	when.
66	
67	Worker and session state paths use `ddxroot.Path()` (shorthand for `ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)`) per `ddx-06cbaa90` (presence-based DDx root resolution and per-project worktree registry).
68

1	---
2	ddx:
3	  id: plan-2026-04-29-artifact-and-run-architecture
4	---
5	# Artifact Generalization & Task Execution
6	
7	Date: 2026-04-29
8	Status: Approved (refined through 3 review rounds with codex + fresh-eyes)
9	
10	## Summary
11	
12	Two threads converge in this plan:
13	
14	1. **Generalize artifacts beyond markdown documents.** Diagrams, wireframes, hero images, prompts — text and binary — become first-class artifacts via media-type plurality and sidecar `.ddx.yaml` files. Generated artifacts are checked into git; regeneration is source-hash-driven.
15	2. **Three-layer run architecture.** DDx owns three explicit layered primitives: `ddx run` (single agent invocation), `ddx try <bead>` (bead attempt in isolated worktree), `ddx work` (queue drain). The current two-class storage split (`ddxroot.Path()/exec-runs/` vs `ddxroot.Path()/executions/<attempt-id>/`) collapses into one substrate.
16	
17	These threads converge: **generating an artifact is a layer-1 agent run** with `produces_artifact` metadata.
18	
19	## Project Identity (ddx-d30bc1a0)
20	
21	All project-scoped run and evidence paths in this plan are rooted at
22	`ddxroot.Path()`, shorthand here for `ddxroot.Path(ctx, projectRoot)`, per
23	`ddx-d30bc1a0`. In-tree projects still resolve to `<projectRoot>/.ddx`;
24	convention-mode projects resolve to the per-project XDG directory derived from
25	project identity. Any remaining literal legacy root-prefixed examples in older
26	plan text should be read as `ddxroot.Path()/...`.
27	
28	## Decisions (resolved through review)
29	
30	- **Artifact authority:** identity present → artifact. No "managed asset" second-class concept.
31	- **FEAT-019 fate:** child of FEAT-010 (not peer). Contracts to evaluation UX; workflow shapes (comparison, replay, benchmark) move to skills library.
32	- **Server posture:** 100% read coverage on HTTP/MCP for all DDx state, sequenced by impact. Writes case-by-case as workflows demand. Only `artifactRegenerate` write surface added in this plan.
33	- **Generated artifacts:** committed to git unconditionally. Size guard (50MB warn / 100MB hard error) with actionable git-lfs guidance; no auto-init / auto-`.gitattributes`.
34	- **CONTRACT-003:** amendments allowed if FEAT-006 work surfaces gaps. The mountable Cobra root export (below) is a load-bearing audit item.
35	- **Run substrate:** single on-disk shape; layer-2 and layer-3 add metadata. `ddxroot.Path()/exec-runs/` and `ddxroot.Path()/executions/<attempt-id>/` collapse.

input.old_string:
This sequence is synchronous from the state-machine perspective: there is no
durable "needs decomposition" status. If implementation needs to release a claim
between steps, it releases to `status=open` with evidence naming the pending
automatic recovery action, not to `blocked`, cooldown, or `proposed`.

## 4. Worker-State Enumeration
input.new_string:
This sequence is synchronous from the state-machine perspective: there is no
durable "needs decomposition" status. If implementation needs to release a claim
between steps, it releases to `status=open` with evidence naming the pending
automatic recovery action, not to `blocked`, cooldown, or `proposed`.

Every transition in this sequence, and every child/sibling/replacement bead it
creates, is written to the same project's `ddxroot.Path()/beads.jsonl`, per
`ddx-06cbaa90` (presence-based DDx root resolution and per-project worktree
registry).
